content-ingestion.title: Content ingestion — backfill podcasts, articles, ainews, LatentSpaceTV (Jan 2025 → today)
content-ingestion.notes:
4 sources: podcasts (@LatentSpacePod), articles (Substack), ainews (smol-ai/ainews), LatentSpaceTV (builders club, paper club, meetups). 204 nodes exist but 0 chunks/embeddings. Existing scripts broken (old schema). Embedding services stubbed. AIE content removed.

Depends on: schema-and-port (DONE), enable-vector-search (DONE)

Phase 0: Enumerate all latent.space Substack articles (Jan 2025 → today)

mcp-server-and-skills.title: MCP server (NPX) + skills/guides — publishable package, shared interface for humans and bots
mcp-server-and-skills.notes:
Make the MCP server an NPX package (like ra-h-mcp-server). Add skills/guides system that ships with the server. Same interface for humans (Claude Code/Desktop) and Discord bots. Bot imports service layer directly.

Depends on: dev-setup-cleanup (DONE), schema-and-port (DONE), enable-vector-search (DONE), content-ingestion
Blocks: discord-bot

discord-bot.title: Discord bots — Sig & Slop, dual-personality bots backed by LS knowledge base
discord-bot.notes:
Two bots from day one. Sig (precise, factual, cites sources) and Slop (unhinged, hot takes, debate starter). Same KB, different personalities.

Architecture: OpenClaw-style Discord gateway — single Node.js process, thread-per-conversation. No complex MD workspace files — bots pull everything from the LS knowledge base. System prompt + KB search = their entire world.

Both react to new content, debate each other, and draw community members into threads.

Depends on: schema-and-port (DONE), enable-vector-search (DONE), content-ingestion, mcp-server-and-skills

tasks.text: Create new repo
Discord.js gateway — single process routing to both bots
Register Sig and Slop as separate Discord applications
Thread-per-conversation model (OpenClaw pattern)

Sig system prompt — precise, factual, cites sources
Slop system prompt — unhinged, hot takes, confrontational
Cross-bot debate: both react to same content in shared threads
Deploy to test Discord server
Demo to swyx
Add to LS Discord

storylines.title: Storylines / narrative arcs — temporal narrative layer connecting content, entities, and claims over time
storylines.notes:
BBC-inspired narrative layer. A storyline is a node_type='storyline' that tracks arcs like 'the scaling laws debate' or 'open vs closed source AI' across episodes, guests, and claims over time. Enables editorial planning and temporal bot queries.

Future — revisit after core graph is populated and bot is live.

Depends on: schema-and-port (DONE), content-ingestion, mcp-server-and-skills

tasks.text: Define storyline metadata schema

Add temporal edge metadata (valid_from, valid_until)
Create initial storylines from existing content
Storyline timeline UI component
Bot can answer 'trace the arc of X across episodes'
Auto-detect emerging storylines from new content
